heaviest_rainfall.status: Record-breaking rain fell on 09 November 2025 with a total accumulation of 47.89mm over 144.8 hours and falling at a rate of 24.6mm per hour at times.

heaviest_rainrate.status: 34.3mm per hour is the maximum rain rate ever recorded on 02 September 2025, during a 15.0 hour storm which dumped 21.19mm of rain from the clouds.

longest_rain.status: The longest rain storm on record lasted 144.8 hours on 09 November 2025 when 47.89mm of rain fell and at one point was falling at 24.6mm per hour.

most_lightning.status: The highest recorded number of lightning strikes in a single day is {highest_count} on {highest_date} since records began on 11 April 2025.

storm.status: Currently no stormy weather.  The most recent storm happened on 18 July 2026 and it lasted for 1 day.

top_10_dry.status: It is currently one of the longest dry periods.  It is the ranked 1st since our records started in 23 September 2024.  There have been 20 consecutive days where it has been dry starting on 03 July 2026.

top_10_rainy.status: The longest rainy period started on 13 January 2026 and lasted for 22 days.

cold_snaps.status: Currently, the weather is stable with no cold snap in progress.  So far, 3 cold snaps have been recorded this year.  The average yearly count is 3.7.

extreme.status: The largest temperature difference recorded was 27.7°C on 24 April 2026.  On that date the temperature reached 23.2°C and fell to -4.5°C.

heatwaves.status: A heatwave started on 2026-07-01 and it has so far lasted for 22 days.  This year has seen 3 heatwaves; the long-term yearly average is 1.9.

tropical.status: The last tropical night was 28 June 2026, there have been only 68 tropical nights since 1973.

average.status: The current average daily wind direction is south-westerly (223°).  In the UK, a south-westerly wind often means mild and wet weather.

gusts.status: The record wind gust remains at 128.2 mph from 27 January 2026.

run.status: The record wind run remains at 1737 miles from 21 October 2012.

speed.status: The highest wind speed ever seen is 93.7 mph on 13 October 2012.

top_10_windy.status: The longest windy period started on 11 October 2012 and lasted for 21 days.

summary.description:
Today, a bit warmer for the feels like temperature (up 1.9°C) and more moisture-laden outside humidity (up 5%).

The past week and month had observable variation in cloud levels (up 780 m), light levels (down 2543 lux), inside humidity (up 6%), and outside temperature (up 1.5°C) when compared to today.

cloud levels.description:
Today’s average cloud levels is 517.7m lower than yesterday, 779.9m lower than the past week, 88.3m lower than the past month.

The cloud levels yesterday fell between 1829m minimum and 6260m maximum, with an average of 3710m; weekly and monthly averages were marginally higher at 4490m and 3800m.
cloud levels.summary: A high cloud base will mean mostly sunny conditions.

summary.summary: Inside; hot for most of the day. Outside; you’ll experience very high heat, extremely taxing outdoors. Also, the air will feel noticeably humid and uncomfortable and winds will be practically absent. Showers, a high cloud base will mean mostly sunny conditions, and sunlight levels will peak at very high intensity. No significant shifts in the weather are expected.

washing.phrase: The conditions are favourable for drying your laundry outside right now but, as always, monitor the situation for changes to the weather.
